symbols.name: n
symbols.def: The number of ordered outcome values.

symbols.name: \mathcal C_{n,l}
symbols.def: The product conditional-law polytope with one normalized conditional cell law for each instrument arm.

symbols.name: q
symbols.def: One probability law of response types, with \(q\ge0\) and \(\mathbf 1^{\mathsf T}q=1\).

symbols.name: c_{n,l}
symbols.def: The ATE loading on response types.

symbols.name: \mathcal P^{\mathrm{IV}}_{n,l}
symbols.def: The class of conditional cell laws admitting a compatible response-type law.

symbols.name: \operatorname{Sig}
symbols.def: The set of tight response-type inequalities.

symbols.name: B
symbols.def: An explicitly encoded proposed activity signature.

symbols.name: \mathsf{VERTREC}_{n,l}
symbols.def: Exact recognition of whether \(B\) is the complete signature of a gauge-fixed vertex.

symbols.name: \mathsf E_{n,l}
symbols.def: A prospective rational-output enumerator of \(\mathcal V_{n,l}\).

symbols.name: w(a,s)
symbols.def: The arm-indexed comparator vector of Boushehrian et al. in their lower-dual sign convention.

symbols.name: \bar w(a,s)
symbols.def: The treatment-swapped negative of the published lower-dual vector.

symbols.name: \mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}
symbols.def: The gauge-fixed upper-dual representatives of the published arm-indexed M-distinct family.

symbols.name: \lambda'
symbols.def: The neighboring vertex in the worked basis exchange.

symbols.name: B^\dagger
symbols.def: The specified ten-equation active basis of \(\lambda^\dagger\).

symbols.name: B'
symbols.def: The canonical basis at the worked neighboring vertex.

symbols.name: S
symbols.def: Treatment-label swapping of observed cell laws.

standard.name: finite response-type IV representation under exclusion, random assignment, and consistency

definitions.name: \(\mathcal C_{n,l}\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal C_{n,l}=\{p\in\mathbb R_+^{\mathcal I_{n,l}}:\sum_{y\in\mathcal Y_n}\sum_{d\in\mathcal D}p_{y,d,z}=1\text{ for every }z\in\mathcal Z_l\}\). Consequently \(\mathbf 1^{\mathsf T}p=l\).

definitions.name: \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{IV}}_{n,l}\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal P^{\mathrm{IV}}_{n,l}=\{p\in\mathcal C_{n,l}:\exists q\in\Delta(\mathcal T_{n,l})\text{ such that }A_{n,l}q=p\}\).

definitions.name: \(\mathcal Q(p)\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal Q(p)=\{q\in\mathbb R_+^{\mathcal T_{n,l}}:A_{n,l}q=p\}\). For \(p\in\mathcal C_{n,l}\), every \(q\in\mathcal Q(p)\) has \(\mathbf1^{\mathsf T}q=1\), since each column of \(A_{n,l}\) contributes once in every arm; the normalization is therefore redundant in this displayed primal system.

definitions.name: \(\Gamma\)
definitions.construction: For \(\lambda\in\mathcal H_{n,l}\), let \(v_z=-\lambda_{(0,0,z)}\) for \(z=2,\ldots,l\), let \(v_1=\sum_{z=2}^l\lambda_{(0,0,z)}\), set \(v_{(y,d,z)}=v_z\), and define \(\Gamma(\lambda)=\lambda+v\). Thus \(\mathcal G_{n,l}=\{\lambda:\lambda_{(0,0,z)}=0\text{ for }z=2,\ldots,l\}\) and \(\mathcal H^0_{n,l}=\mathcal H_{n,l}\cap\mathcal G_{n,l}\).

definitions.name: \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}\)
definitions.construction: For \(a\in\mathcal Z_l\) and nonconstant \(s=(s_0,\ldots,s_{n-1})\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\), define \(w(a,s)_{y,1,j}=0\) for \(j\ne a\), \(w(a,s)_{y,1,a}=y-(n-1)\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,j}=0\) for \(j\notin\{a,s_y\}\), \(w(a,s)_{y,0,s_y}=n-1-y\), and \(w(a,s)_{y,0,a}=-(n-1)\). The paper states this family in the lower-dual convention. In the upper-dual convention define \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}=\{\Gamma(\bar w(a,s)):a\in\mathcal Z_l,\ s\in(\mathcal Z_l\setminus\{a\})^n\text{ nonconstant},\ \bar w(a,s)_{y,d,z}=-w(a,s)_{y,1-d,z}\}\). The indexing list has \(l((l-1)^n-(l-1))\) entries, but different indices can define the same vector because \(s_{n-1}\) multiplies a zero coefficient. As a set of distinct literal coefficient vectors, \(|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,2}|=0\) and \(|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}|=l(l-1)^{n-1}\) for \(l\ge3\).

definitions.name: \((B^\dagger,B',\lambda')\)
definitions.construction: Order types lexicographically by the treatment word and then by \((\eta(0),\eta(1))\). Let \(B^\dagger=\{((000),(11)),((001),(10)),((001),(11)),((010),(00)),((011),(00)),((011),(10)),((100),(01)),((100),(11)),((110),(00)),((110),(01))\}\). Replace the leaving equation \(((001),(10))\) by the entering equation \(((010),(10))\), retain the other nine equations of \(B^\dagger\), and append the gauges \(\lambda_{(0,0,2)}=\lambda_{(0,0,3)}=0\). The ten type equations have rank \(10\); with the two gauges the system has rank \(12\) and solution with blocks \((2,1,2,1)\), \((0,-1,-2,0)\), \((0,0,0,0)\), denoted \(\lambda'\). The armwise separable slack minimum is nonnegative, and its equality words give exact signature \(\{((000),(11)),((001),(11)),((010),(00)),((010),(10)),((011),(00)),((011),(10)),((100),(01)),((100),(11)),((101),(01)),((101),(11)),((110),(00)),((110),(01)),((111),(00)),((111),(01))\}\). The lexicographically least rank basis in this signature is \(B'=\{((000),(11)),((001),(11)),((010),(00)),((010),(10)),((011),(00)),((011),(10)),((100),(01)),((100),(11)),((110),(00)),((110),(01))\}\). Since \(B^\dagger\prec B'\), the proposed decreasing-basis orientation directs this edge from \(B'\) to \(B^\dagger\).

definitions.name: \(\mathsf E_{n,l}\) handle
definitions.construction: Encode a node by the lexicographically least ordered set of \(r=2nl-l+1\) independent tight type equations on \(\mathcal G_{n,l}\); equivalently, retain those \(r\) equations together with the \(l-1\) fixed gauge equations for an ambient rank-\(2nl\) certificate. Encode \(\tau=(\delta,\eta)\) by the \(l\)-bit word \((\delta(1),\ldots,\delta(l))\) and the two labels \((\eta(0),\eta(1))\). Reconstruct the rational vertex by exact linear algebra. For one type-equation exchange, parameterize the resulting affine line; for each \((\eta(0),\eta(1))\), minimize slack over \(\delta\) armwise as \(\sum_{z=1}^l\min_{d\in\mathcal D}\lambda_{(\eta(d),d,z)}-(\eta(1)-\eta(0))\), enumerate its minimizing treatment words at equality, and thereby test feasibility, list the complete signature, and reject rank failure or unlisted tight types without expanding \(2^l n^2\) types. A candidate root is \(\Gamma(\bar w(1,(2,\ldots,2,3)))\). The required parent map chooses, among recognized one-exchange neighbors whose canonical basis is lexicographically smaller, the lexicographically first; its proof obligations are uniqueness, acyclicity, reachability to the root, and polynomially many local tests. Inputs are \(1^n,1^l\); delay counts bit operations, basis and type encodings, and rational output. The integer zero-one equation matrix, ATE right sides bounded by \(n-1\), and determinant bounds control numerator and denominator lengths.

definitions.name: \(U(p),L(p)\)
definitions.construction: \(U(p)=\max_{q\in\mathcal Q(p)}c_{n,l}^{\mathsf T}q=\min_{\lambda\in\mathcal V_{n,l}}\lambda^{\mathsf T}p\), and \(L(p)=-U(S(p))\) where \(S(p)_{y,d,z}=p_{y,1-d,z}\).

statements.statement: \(K_{n,l}=\{v:v_{(y,d,z)}=a_z\text{ for all }(y,d,z)\in\mathcal I_{n,l},\sum_{z=1}^l a_z=0\}\), so \(\dim K_{n,l}=l-1\). For every \(p\in\mathcal C_{n,l}\) and \(v\in K_{n,l}\), \(v^{\mathsf T}p=\sum_z a_z=0\). The map \(\Gamma\) is constant on each \(\sim\)-class and that class meets \(\mathcal G_{n,l}\) once. Hence \(\mathcal H^0_{n,l}\) is pointed and its vertices biject with the M-distinct vertices of \(\mathcal H_{n,l}\).
statements.justification: The gauge removes the exact dual lineality and makes signatures, vertices, and observed-law functionals canonical.
statements.gap: Without the transversal, the upper-dual polyhedron has no ordinary vertices because of its \(l-1\)-dimensional lineality.
statements.consumer: The recognition algorithm and all exact rational vertex certificates operate in this pointed section.

statements.statement: For every \(p\in\mathcal P^{\mathrm{IV}}_{n,l}\subseteq\mathcal C_{n,l}\), \(\{c_{n,l}^{\mathsf T}q:q\in\mathcal Q(p)\}=[L(p),U(p)]\), with \(U(p)=\min_{\lambda\in\mathcal V_{n,l}}\lambda^{\mathsf T}p\) and \(L(p)=-U(S(p))\). The primal equality \(A_{n,l}q=p\) already implies \(\mathbf1^{\mathsf T}q=1\) arm by arm, so this is the dual of the normalization-free displayed primal system.
statements.justification: This recalled LP-duality fact explains why a complete vertex catalogue gives explicit sharp endpoints.
statements.gap: It is established background from Balke--Pearl finite response-type duality and the dual formulation used by Boushehrian et al.; it is not a claimed contribution.
statements.consumer: It translates any successful enumeration into sharp all-arm ATE evaluation.

statements.statement: For \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\), can \(\mathsf{VERTREC}_{n,l}(B)\) be decided in time polynomial in \(n+l+|B|\), including a certificate that the listed tight types are complete, without expanding \(2^l n^2\) response types?
statements.justification: Exact signature recognition is the local oracle that distinguishes a vertex from a merely feasible active basis.
statements.gap: Boushehrian et al. prove a binary-instrument signature bijection, but no multivalued exact-signature oracle is supplied.
statements.consumer: It verifies nodes and degenerate canonicalizations in an IV-specific enumeration algorithm.

statements.statement: For \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\), let \(P\) be the explicit polynomial-time parent map with root \(\lambda^\star\) furnished by the global-parent theorem. Can one construct a deterministic rational-output child generator which, on input \(\mu\in\mathcal V_{n,l}\), lists exactly the adjacent vertices \(\nu\) satisfying \(P(\nu)=\mu\) with bit-operation delay polynomial in \(n+l\), reports exhaustion in polynomial time after its last child, and can be coupled to a traversal that lists every element of \(\mathcal V_{n,l}\) exactly once with global bit-operation delay polynomial in \(n+l\), including polynomial-time termination after the final vertex? As one sufficient route, can the relevant finite extreme rays of the tangent cone at each \(\mu\), their exact finite line-search endpoints, and the parent-filtered subset be generated with these delay and exhaustion guarantees without expanding all \(2^l n^2\) type inequalities or all active bases?
statements.justification: A complete deterministic child iterator would turn the finite sharp-endpoint representation into an unconditional polynomial-delay analytical enumerator.
statements.gap: The inverse-child generation and polynomial-time exhaustion guarantees remain open at degenerate vertices, despite the global parent map, compressed verifier, simple-vertex algorithm, and \(\mathsf{NP}\)-oracle upper bound.
statements.consumer: Vertex-enumeration software and multi-arm IV sharp-bound routines need this residual resolved before the note can claim unconditional polynomial-delay listing of every coefficient vector.

statements.statement: Let \(N_n=5\cdot4^{n-1}-2^{n+2}+4\). Among the complementary pairs \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\) satisfying \(2(l-1)^{n-2}\ge N_n-2\), which are not certified by the sufficient condition \(2(l-1)^{n-2}<N_n-2\), for which pairs can one establish \(|\mathcal V_{n,l}|>|\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}|=l(l-1)^{n-1}\), and thereby certify strict incompleteness of the literal family \(\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{n,l}\)? In particular, does the strict inequality hold for every \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\)?
statements.justification: The essential-pair lower bound settles the strict-deficit claim under an explicit sufficient inequality, so the residual question concerns the complementary parameter pairs.
statements.gap: It remains unknown from the present certificates whether every pair with \(2(l-1)^{n-2}\ge N_n-2\) still has vertices outside the literal comparator family.
statements.consumer: The answer determines where empirical users can certify, without exhaustive enumeration, that the arm-indexed forms omit sharp ATE endpoint functionals.

sampling_model.design: The proposal is identification and enumeration based; its finite LP background uses \(p\in\mathcal C_{n,l}\), the product of arm-specific conditional cell-law simplices.
sampling_model.overlap: Every retained instrument arm has positive probability.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: Empirical \(p\) consists of within-\(Z=z\) conditional frequencies, each summing to one; no sampling-limit theorem is claimed.

bibliography.citation: A. Balke and J. Pearl (1997). Bounds on treatment effects from studies with imperfect compliance. \textit{Journal of the American Statistical Association} 92, 1171--1176. doi:10.1080/01621459.1997.10474074.

A. Boushehrian, M. R. Badri, S. Akbari, and N. Kiyavash (2026). Fundamental Limits and Optimal Methods for Sharp Analytical Causal Bounds in Instrumental Variable Models. \textit{arXiv:2604.12802}, Theorem 4 and Appendix D.1.

Y. Song, F. R. Guo, K. C. G. Chan, and T. S. Richardson (2024). The Categorical Instrumental Variable Model: Characterization, Partial Identification, and Statistical Inference. \textit{arXiv:2405.09510}.
D. Avis and K. Fukuda (1996). Reverse search for enumeration. \textit{Discrete Applied Mathematics} 65, 21--46. doi:10.1016/0166-218X(95)00026-N.

L. Khachiyan, E. Boros, K. Borys, K. Elbassioni, and V. Gurvich (2008). Generating all vertices of a polyhedron is hard. \textit{Discrete and Computational Geometry} 39, 174--190. doi:10.1007/s00454-008-9050-5.
V. Chernozhukov, S. Lee, and A. M. Rosen (2013). Intersection Bounds: Estimation and Inference. \textit{Econometrica} 81, 667--737. doi:10.3982/ECTA8718.
B. Aspvall and R. E. Stone (1980). Khachiyan's Linear Programming Algorithm. \textit{Journal of Algorithms} 1, 1--13. doi:10.1016/0196-6774(80)90002-4.

tldr:
In the finite response-type IV model with binary treatment, ordered \(n\)-point outcomes, \(l\) instrument arms, and no monotonicity, the gauge-fixed upper-dual section removes the \((l-1)\)-dimensional lineality and indexes the sharp ATE endpoint by M-distinct vertices. Exact activity signatures are recognizable in polynomial time, and the published arm-indexed family is strictly incomplete for every \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\). For binary outcomes, the strongest uniform certificate is
\[|\mathcal V_{2,l}|\ge6\binom l2+9\binom l3=\frac32l^2(l-1),\qquad |\mathcal V_{2,l}\setminus\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,l}|\ge\frac{l(l-1)(3l-2)}2.\]
Its \(l=3\) specialization gives the certified lower bounds \(27\) and \(21\). These are lower bounds, not exact multi-arm counts. The sharp endpoints are finite minima and maxima of observable linear functionals, while unconditional deterministic polynomial-delay enumeration for arbitrary \((n,l)\) remains open.

project_justification.gap: For multi-valued instruments, existing compatibility characterizations and analytical comparator constructions do not provide a complete list of M-distinct quotient-dual vertices. Generic inequality-based vertex enumeration expands a response-type system with \(2^l n^2\) inequalities. The unresolved algorithmic gap is specifically unconditional deterministic inverse-child generation with polynomial delay and polynomial-time exhaustion at degenerate vertices; it is not compatibility characterization or numerical linear-program identification.
project_justification.niche: The note develops exact vertex calculus on the gauge-fixed section \(\mathcal H^0_{n,l}\). It combines a polynomial-time exact-signature recognizer, an explicit decreasing-objective parent map, compressed tangent-ray verification, universal comparator-deficit certificates, and support-preserving zero-arm lifts. For \(n=2\), disjoint essential-arm supports place six certified vertices on every unordered arm pair and nine further certified vertices on every unordered arm triple.
project_justification.fill:
The contribution supplies actionable finite linear-functional representations of the sharp ATE endpoints indexed by \(\mathcal V_{n,l}\), proves that the literal Boushehrian--Badri--Akbari--Kiyavash family is strictly incomplete for all \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\), retains the universal additive deficit theorem, and establishes
\[|\mathcal V_{2,l}|\ge\frac32l^2(l-1),\qquad |\mathcal V_{2,l}\setminus\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,l}|\ge\frac{l(l-1)(3l-2)}2.\]
It also isolates the sole enumeration residual: converting polynomial-time parent evaluation and candidate-ray verification into an unconditional deterministic child iterator for nonsimple vertices.

related_work: Boushehrian--Badri--Akbari--Kiyavash (arXiv:2604.12802) provide an arm-indexed family of discrete-IV dual vertices. The present note uses their multi-valued theorem for vertexhood but does not import its literal distinct-index count: the final response-word coordinate has zero coefficient, and the project proves the corrected literal coefficient-vector count from effective prefixes. Song--Guo--Chan--Richardson (2024, arXiv:2405.09510), Theorem 1, characterize compatibility of an observed categorical-IV law and a joint counterfactual distribution by necessary and sufficient inequalities. Their partial-identification procedure bounds ATE and other linear functionals by optimizing over that joint counterfactual polytope using generic linear or convex programs. This does not subsume the present theorem: Song et al. do not form the quotient lower- or upper-dual polyhedron modulo its kernel, enumerate its M-distinct vertices, classify those vertices by exact essential-arm support, or prove omitted-family deficit certificates. In particular, their inequalities and optimization prescription do not imply the disjoint \(6\binom l2\) pair-essential and \(9\binom l3\) triple-essential vertex constructions, the bound \(|\mathcal V_{2,l}|\ge\frac32l^2(l-1)\), or the comparator deficit \(\frac{l(l-1)(3l-2)}2\). Conversely, this note does not replace Song et al.'s compatibility characterization or inference theory. The unresolved gap claimed here is only complete quotient-dual vertex enumeration with unconditional deterministic polynomial delay, not characterization or numerical linear-program identification of the compatible counterfactual polytope. The binary-instrument count is used as a consistency input and lifting seed, not promoted to an exact multi-arm counting formula.
interpretation:
The causal target is \(\theta=\mathbb E[Y(1)-Y(0)]\), whereas the observable identifying object is the sharp interval \([L(p),U(p)]\). Under the finite response-type IV representation,
\[U(p)=\min_{\lambda\in\mathcal V_{n,l}}\lambda^{\mathsf T}p,\qquad L(p)=-U(S(p)).\]
The cardinality results show that even with binary outcomes the analytical index set is much richer than the literal comparator family: for every \(l\ge3\), there are at least \(6\binom l2\) pair-essential and \(9\binom l3\) triple-essential M-classes, while every \(n=2\) comparator has only two essential arms. Thus optimizing only over the published arm-indexed forms can omit sharp inequalities that genuinely combine three instrument arms. Once a complete finite vertex list is supplied, empirical cell frequencies can be inserted into these linear functionals and established finite intersection-bound or max-of-means methods may be applied; no new sampling, limit-law, or coverage theorem is claimed here.
technical_internal_limitation: Unconditional deterministic polynomial-delay generation of all vertices remains unresolved. The core proves polynomial-time exact-signature recognition, an explicit polynomial-time global parent map, polynomial verification of supplied compressed tangent rays and their child endpoints, polynomial child generation at simple vertices, and a polynomial-delay construction with an \(\mathsf{NP}\) oracle. It also proves that the root can have exponentially many active types, that a canonical one-equation exchange can miss an adjacent vertex, and that a natural extended formulation can have exponentially many auxiliary vertices over one projected ray. These facts delimit the remaining obstacle but do not prove impossibility. The note therefore makes no unrelativized polynomial-delay claim.
honest_scope:
The identification result is conditional only on the stated finite response-type IV model, encompassing exclusion, randomized instrument assignment, and consistency; no treatment monotonicity is imposed. The section and duality arguments deliver the sharp population interval, not a new sampling or coverage theorem. The note proves polynomial-time activity-signature recognition, comparator incompleteness for every \(n\ge2\) and \(l\ge3\), the universal additive-support lower bound, and the binary-outcome bounds
\[|\mathcal V_{2,l}|\ge\frac32l^2(l-1),\qquad |\mathcal V_{2,l}\setminus\mathcal F^{\mathrm{BBAK}}_{2,l}|\ge\frac{l(l-1)(3l-2)}2.\]
It does not claim that either expression is an exact count, does not claim a closed counting formula for \(|\mathcal V_{n,l}|\) when \(l>2\), does not claim a new estimator or inference theorem, and does not claim an unconditional deterministic polynomial-delay enumerator. The exact \((2,3)\) lower-bound specialization remains \(27/21\), and deterministic oracle removal at nonsimple vertices remains the sole acknowledged enumeration residual.
